messages.content: You are the Mise assistant. Mise is an offline-first meal-planner PWA: weekly plans (plans/<week>.json with stacking entries, pinned/out flags, a buffer snack), a recipe bank (per-serving nutrition, staple-flagged ingredients, Daily Dozen foodGroups), a pantry with shelf-life auto-expiry, and a derived shopping list. You navigate, extract, and fill in app data; you never edit the app's code. Never invent nutrition numbers: values you cannot compute from given data need re-estimation or a greger_audit tool call. To use a tool, reply with exactly one fenced json block: {"tool": <name>, "args": {...}}.
How much protein is in Apple Orange Almond Butter Plate?
32g protein per serving (570 kcal, 54g carbs, 28g fat). The recipe makes 1 servings; values are per serving and method is "estimated".

Rescale Apricot Banana Cottage Cheese Plate from 1 to 2 servings and give me the full corrected recipe JSON.

Scaled every ingredient by 2/1; per-serving nutrition is unchanged by rescaling.
```json
{
  "id": "apricot-banana-cottage-cheese-plate",
  "name": "Apricot Banana Cottage Cheese Plate",
  "description": "Fresh apricot and banana with cottage cheese and walnuts, a fruit-forward snack plate with real protein for the gain phase.",
  "servings": 2,
  "prepTime": 5,
  "cookTime": 0,
  "totalTime": 5,
  "mealType": "snack",
  "cuisine": "american",
  "tags": [
    "fruit-forward",
    "no-cook",
    "quick"
  ],
  "difficulty": 1,
  "purpose": [
    "everyday"
  ],
  "effort": "assembly",
  "ingredients": [
    {
      "qty": 8,
      "unit": "each",
      "food": "apricot",
      "note": "fresh, halved and pitted",
      "staple": false
    },
    {
      "qty": 2,
      "unit": "each",
      "food": "banana",
      "note": "sliced",
      "staple": false
    },
    {
      "qty": 1.5,
      "unit": "cup",
      "food": "cottage cheese",
      "note": "low-fat",
      "staple": false
    },
    {
      "qty": 0.5,
      "unit": "cup",
      "food": "walnuts",
      "note": "chopped",
      "staple": false
    },
    {
      "qty": 2,
      "unit": "tbsp",
      "food": "honey",
      "optional": true,
      "staple": false
    },
    {
      "qty": 0.5,
      "unit": "tsp",
      "food": "cinnamon",
      "optional": true,
      "staple": false
    }
  ],
  "instructions": [
    {
      "step": 1,
      "text": "Halve and pit the apricots; slice the banana."
    },
    {
      "step": 2,
      "text": "Spoon cottage cheese onto a plate."
    },
    {
      "step": 3,
      "text": "Arrange the apricot and banana alongside."
    },
    {
      "step": 4,
      "text": "Scatter walnuts over the top."
    },
    {
      "step": 5,
      "text": "Drizzle with honey and dust with cinnamon if using; eat right away."
    }
  ],
  "nutrition": {
    "calories": 610,
    "protein": 30,
    "carbs": 70,
    "fat": 28,
    "method": "estimated"
  },
  "foodGroups": {
    "beans": 0,
    "berries": 0,
    "otherFruit": 2,
    "cruciferousVeg": 0,
    "greens": 0,
    "otherVeg": 0,
    "flaxseed": 0,
    "nuts": 1,
    "spicesHerbs": 0,
    "wholeGrains": 0,
    "beverages": 0,
    "method": "estimated"
  },
  "timesCooked": 0,
  "lessons": []
}
```

Rescale Banana Oat Pancakes from 2 to 5 servings and give me the full corrected recipe JSON.

Scaled every ingredient by 5/2; per-serving nutrition is unchanged by rescaling.
```json
{
  "id": "banana-oat-pancakes",
  "name": "Banana Oat Pancakes",
  "description": "Blender-batter pancakes: banana, oats, eggs, and a scoop of whey. Easy-digesting carbs and clean protein 1-2 hours before tennis or climbing.",
  "servings": 5,
  "prepTime": 5,
  "cookTime": 10,
  "totalTime": 15,
  "mealType": "breakfast",
  "cuisine": "american",
  "tags": [
    "blender-batter",
    "high-protein"
  ],
  "difficulty": 1,
  "purpose": [
    "pre-activity",
    "everyday"
  ],
  "effort": "cook",
  "ingredients": [
    {
      "qty": 5,
      "unit": "each",
      "food": "banana",
      "note": "ripe"
    },
    {
      "qty": 2.5,
      "unit": "cup",
      "food": "rolled oats",
      "staple": true
    },
    {
      "qty": 7.5,
      "unit": "each",
      "food": "egg",
      "note": "large"
    },
    {
      "qty": 2.5,
      "unit": "scoop",
      "food": "whey protein powder",
      "note": "vanilla or unflavored",
      "staple": true
    },
    {
      "qty": 1.25,
      "unit": "tsp",
      "food": "baking powder"
    },
    {
      "qty": 0.63,
      "unit": "tsp",
      "food": "vanilla extract",
      "staple": true
    },
    {
      "qty": 2.5,
      "unit": "pinch",
      "food": "salt",
      "staple": true
    },
    {
      "qty": 2.5,
      "unit": "tsp",
      "food": "butter",
      "note": "for the griddle",
      "staple": true
    },
    {
      "qty": 2.5,
      "unit": "tbsp",
      "food": "ground flaxseed",
      "note": "blend into the batter for lignans and ALA omega-3",
      "optional": true,
      "staple": false
    },
    {
      "qty": 2.5,
      "unit": "cup",
      "food": "mixed berries",
      "note": "to serve on top",
      "optional": true,
      "staple": false
    }
  ],
  "instructions": [
    {
      "step": 1,
      "text": "Blend banana, oats, eggs, whey, baking powder, vanilla, and salt until smooth."
    },
    {
      "step": 2,
      "text": "Let the batter sit 2-3 min to thicken slightly."
    },
    {
      "step": 3,
      "text": "Melt a little butter on a nonstick griddle over medium heat."
    },
    {
      "step": 4,
      "text": "Pour small pancakes (~3 in) and cook 2 min until bubbles set, flip, cook 1-2 min more."
    },
    {
      "step": 5,
      "text": "Repeat with remaining batter; serve warm."
    }
  ],
  "nutrition": {
    "calories": 420,
    "protein": 30,
    "carbs": 48,
    "fat": 12,
    "method": "estimated"
  },
  "foodGroups": {
    "beans": 0,
    "berries": 1,
    "otherFruit": 1,
    "cruciferousVeg": 0,
    "greens": 0,
    "otherVeg": 0,
    "flaxseed": 0.5,
    "nuts": 0,
    "spicesHerbs": 0,
    "wholeGrains": 2,
    "beverages": 0,
    "method": "estimated"
  },
  "timesCooked": 0,
  "lessons": [
    "Greger vet: 0.5 cup dry oats per serving = 1 cup cooked = 2 whole grain servings."
  ]
}
```

}
